story.iWant: number line placement drills that help me visualize quantities
story.soThat: I improve my number sense through repeated practice

task.subtask: Implement horizontal number line UI (280px min, 90% viewport max)
Add range indicators (0 and 100/1000 labels)
Implement draggable marker (44px tap target, Framer Motion drag="x")
Add target number display ("Where is 47?")
Implement Submit button with disabled state logic
Add optional timer display
Calculate position percentage: (tapX - lineStartX) / lineWidth * range
Implement ±10% tolerance for correctness
Generate drill result with accuracy, timeToAnswer, correctness
Correct answer: Green flash, success sound (if enabled), +1 animation
Incorrect answer: Show both correct position (gray) and user position (red)
Auto-advance to next drill after 1.5 seconds
Easy (0-100 range, multiples of 10 targets) for first 2 drills
Medium (0-100 range, any number) for next 3 drills
Hard (0-1000 range) if user accuracy > 80% on medium
Keyboard support: arrow keys move marker, Enter submits
ARIA labels for screen readers
Respect prefers-reduced-motion
Component tests: rendering, user interaction, drag behavior
Unit tests: position calculation, accuracy tolerance, difficulty logic
Integration tests: result persistence to Dexie drill_results table
Accessibility tests: keyboard navigation, screen reader support

criterion.given: a training session is active with Number Line drill selected (Story 3.1 complete)
criterion.then: The drill displays: - Horizontal number line spanning screen width (280px minimum, 90% viewport max) - Range indicators: "0" on left, "100" on right (or "0" to "1000" for harder levels) - Target number displayed above line: "Where is 47?" - Draggable marker (🔴 red dot, 44px tap target) that user positions - "Submit" button (disabled until marker positioned) - Timer showing elapsed time (optional, can hide for reduced pressure)

criterion.steps: 1. User taps/drags marker to position on number line 2. Position calculates as percentage: (tapX - lineStartX) / lineWidth * range 3. User clicks "Submit" 4. Visual feedback: - Correct (within ±10% tolerance): Green flash, success sound (if enabled), "+1" animation - Incorrect: Shows correct position briefly (gray marker), shows user's position (red marker) 5. Auto-advances to next drill after 1.5 seconds

criterion.description: Drill result recorded to Dexie drill_results table
criterion.dataModel: { id: uuid(), sessionId: currentSessionId, timestamp: Date.now(), module: 'number_line', targetNumber: 47, userAnswer: 45, correctAnswer: 47, accuracy: 95.7, // Percentage (100 - error%) timeToAnswer: 3247, // milliseconds difficulty: 'medium' }

criterion.progression: - First 2 drills: Easy (0-100 range, multiples of 10 as targets) - Next 3 drills: Medium (0-100 range, any number) - Remaining: Hard (0-1000 range) IF user accuracy > 80% on medium

criterion.requirements: - Arrow keys move marker - Enter key submits answer - Proper ARIA labels - 44px minimum tap target (WCAG 2.1 AA)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Training & Drill Engine
doc.snippet: Number Line Drill: Visual number placement drill on 0-100 or 0-1000 range. Drill presents challenge, user interacts and submits answer, drill calculates result (accuracy, time, correctness). Marker: Framer Motion motion.div with drag="x" constraint. Tolerance calculation: Math.abs(userAnswer - correctAnswer) / range <= 0.1

doc.title: Epic 3: Training & Drill Engine
doc.snippet: Number line placement drills that help visualize quantities. Horizontal number line spanning screen width, draggable marker, ±10% tolerance for correctness, visual and audio feedback.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.snippet: Number line manipulatives for drill interactions. Clean professional design builds trust, bright rounded aesthetics reduce math anxiety, balance credibility with playful stress-reducing visual design.

doc.title: Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: Primary feature module: src/features/training/ with components/, drills/, hooks/, content/ subdirectories. Drill components implement common interface DrillProps.

artifact.reason: Main session orchestrator - calls individual drill components. NumberLineDrill will be rendered by this component when drill type is 'number_line' from drillQueue.
Protected route that wraps TrainingSession. Ensures assessment exists before allowing training access.
Extended with training state including drillQueue, currentDrillIndex, results. NumberLineDrill will call onComplete() to update results array.
Weighted drill selection algorithm that generates drillQueue including 'number_line' drill types based on training plan weights.
Data model definitions. NumberLineDrill will persist results to DrillResult interface with number line specific fields (targetNumber, userAnswer, correctAnswer).
Reusable keypad component from Epic 2. Can potentially be referenced for input patterns, but NumberLineDrill uses drag interaction instead.

package.name: framer-motion
package.usage: Draggable marker with drag='x' constraint, feedback animations, confetti

package.name: dexie
package.usage: Persist drill results to drill_results table

package.name: tailwindcss
package.usage: Styling number line, marker, visual feedback

package.name: vitest
package.usage: Unit tests for position calculation, accuracy tolerance

package.name: @playwright/test
package.usage: E2E testing (Story 3.8 will test number line drill integration)

constraint.rule: NumberLineDrill must implement DrillProps interface: { difficulty: 'easy' | 'medium' | 'hard', sessionId: string, onComplete: (result: DrillResult) => void }
Use HTML div with click/touch handlers, NOT Canvas (better accessibility)
Marker: Framer Motion <motion.div> with drag="x" constraint, not free 2D dragging
Drag response must be immediate (<16ms frame time). Use CSS transform, not left/top positioning. No jank during marker dragging.
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance: 44px minimum tap target for marker, keyboard navigation support, ARIA labels
Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query - disable animations if user has motion sensitivity
All drill results must persist immediately after completion (no batching). Wrap Dexie writes in try-catch with localStorage fallback.
100% test coverage required per architecture specification. Manual verification in dev server mandatory before marking story 'done'.

interface.name: DrillProps
interface.usage: NumberLineDrill must implement this interface. TrainingSession provides props, drill calls onComplete with DrillResult.

interface.name: DrillResult (Number Line specific)
interface.usage: NumberLineDrill creates this object on Submit and passes to onComplete(). Persisted to Dexie drill_results table.

interface.name: SessionContext.startTrainingSession
interface.signature: startTrainingSession(sessionId: string, sessionType: 'quick' | 'full', drillQueue: DrillType[]): void
interface.usage: Called by TrainingSession before drills start. drillQueue includes 'number_line' drill types from weighted selection.

interface.name: Dexie drill_results table
interface.usage: NumberLineDrill persists results after user submits answer. Query indexed by sessionId for session summary.

interface.name: Framer Motion drag constraint
interface.signature:
<motion.div
          drag="x"
          dragConstraints={{ left: 0, right: lineWidth }}
          dragElastic={0}
          dragMomentum={false}
          onDragEnd={(event, info) => updateMarkerPosition(info.point.x)}
        />
interface.usage: Marker component uses Framer Motion for smooth, constrained horizontal dragging. dragConstraints prevents marker from leaving number line bounds.

tests.standards: Testing standards per architecture.md and Epic 3 tech spec: - 100% test coverage required (statements, branches, functions, lines) - Component tests use React Testing Library with accessibility-focused queries - Unit tests for position calculation, accuracy tolerance, difficulty progression logic - Integration tests verify Dexie persistence and SessionContext interaction - Accessibility tests for keyboard navigation and ARIA labels - Visual regression tests optional (deferred) - Manual verification in running dev server MANDATORY before marking 'done' (Epic 2 retrospective lesson)
tests.locations: - src/features/training/drills/NumberLineDrill.test.tsx (component tests) - src/features/training/drills/NumberLineDrill.integration.test.tsx (integration with Dexie) - tests/e2e/training-flow.spec.ts (E2E test in Story 3.8 will include number line drill)

ideas.testIdea: Test NumberLineDrill renders with correct UI elements: - Verify number line element exists with 280px min width - Verify range labels (0 and 100/1000) display correctly based on difficulty - Verify target number displays ("Where is 47?") - Verify marker renders with 44px tap target size - Verify Submit button is disabled initially, enabled after marker positioned - Test different difficulty levels render correct ranges

Test user interaction flow: - Simulate drag marker to position on number line - Verify position calculates correctly: (tapX - lineStartX) / lineWidth * range - Click Submit button - Verify correct answer shows green flash animation - Verify incorrect answer shows both gray (correct) and red (user) markers - Verify auto-advance after 1.5 seconds
Unit test position calculation logic: - Test edge cases: marker at 0%, 50%, 100% of line width - Test calculation for 0-100 range vs 0-1000 range - Test rounding behavior for calculated answers

Test drill result persistence: - Complete drill with correct answer - Verify DrillResult object created with all required fields - Verify onComplete() called with correct result - Verify result persisted to Dexie drill_results table - Query Dexie to confirm sessionId, module, accuracy, timeToAnswer stored

Test accuracy calculation: - Test ±10% tolerance threshold - userAnswer = 47, targetNumber = 47 → accuracy = 100% - userAnswer = 45, targetNumber = 47 → accuracy ~95.7% - userAnswer = 38, targetNumber = 47 → accuracy ~81% - userAnswer = 30, targetNumber = 47 → accuracy = 70%

Test difficulty progression: - Mock session with first 2 drills at easy difficulty - Verify easy drills use 0-100 range with multiples of 10 targets - Mock drills 3-5 at medium difficulty - Verify medium drills use 0-100 range with any number targets - Mock user achieving >80% accuracy on medium drills - Verify next number_line drill uses hard difficulty (0-1000 range)

Test keyboard navigation: - Focus on marker with Tab key - Press ArrowLeft/ArrowRight to move marker - Verify marker position updates - Press Enter to submit answer - Verify keyboard interaction works identically to mouse/touch

Test ARIA labels and screen reader support: - Verify number line has aria-label describing interaction - Verify marker has aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow - Verify target number has aria-live region for screen reader announcement - Test with screen reader simulation (testing-library/jest-dom)

Visual regression test (optional): - Screenshot number line on mobile (320px), tablet (768px), desktop (1024px) - Verify responsive layout doesn't break - Test marker appearance at different positions
Performance test: - Measure drag response time with performance.now() - Verify dragEnd handler completes in <16ms - Test on simulated low-end device (CPU throttling in DevTools)
Test error scenarios: - Dexie write failure → verify localStorage fallback - Missing sessionId prop → verify error boundary catches - Invalid difficulty prop → verify component handles gracefully
Test prefers-reduced-motion: - Mock matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') - Verify feedback animations are disabled - Verify functionality still works without animations
